server_binary: Path to the llama-server executable file.
model_file: Select the large language model file (.gguf) to use for captioning.
projector_file: Select the multimodal projector file (.gguf) for vision capabilities.
context: Total context window size (tokens). Go as high as your VRAM allows
gpu_layers: Number of model layers to offload to GPU. Lower if 'out of memory'.
kill_gpu: Forcefully terminate all processes currently using the GPU.
start_server: Launch the llama-server with the specified configuration.
stop_server: Gracefully stop the currently running llama-server.
add_folder: Add an image folder to the batch processing queue.
start_batch: Start or stop captioning all images in the queue.
overwrite: If enabled, existing .txt caption files will be overwritten.
load_editor: Select a folder to manually review and edit image captions.
filter_editor: Search and display only images whose captions contain this text.
clear_filter: Reset the search filter and show all images in the folder.
filter_src: Folder containing the image/caption pairs you want to filter.
filter_kw: Keyword to search for in the .txt caption files.
filter_tgt: Destination folder where matching image/caption pairs will be moved.
filter_move: Execute the move operation for all pairs containing the keyword.
crop_in: Folder containing images to be automatically cropped.
crop_out: Folder where the cropped images will be saved.
crop_conform: Snap the longest side to a multiple of 256.
crop_margin: Extra padding added to the detected box before snapping (in percent of object size).
crop_min_side: The resulting crop's longest side will be at least this many pixels.
crop_square: Forces the crop into a 1:1 aspect ratio. The shorter side will be expanded to match the longest side, within image boundaries. Will attempt to reach Min Side even for small source images.
crop_one_per: When multiple objects are detected in an image, save only the largest (best) crop per image instead of all detections.
